2605.23311.title: DART: Semantic Recoverability for Structured Tool Agents
2605.23311.paper_summary: When a structured tool agent fails mid-execution, the runtime faces a dilemma: replaying the entire task is safe but wasteful, while restoring from a local checkpoint is efficient but can leave committed downstream work tied to an upstream history that no longer exists. This tension is acute in commitment-sensitive settings, where rollback targets a single failed instance yet downstream consumers have already acted on its output. Existing recovery approaches provide mechanical rollback but no criterion for whether a local restore remains semantically valid after downstream commitment. We formalize this gap as semantic recoverability and address it in DART, a modular runtime that localizes the failed instance, certifies semantically recoverable boundaries of that instance, aligns checkpoints to those boundaries, and selects an admissible restore point that preserves committed downstream work under dependency and effect constraints-or blocks otherwise. Across three LLM-driven domains and external validation on a LangGraph-based substrate, DART correctly recovers all evaluated commitment-sensitive cases where baseline local recovery fails, and a five-domain safety audit finds no unsafe admitted rollbacks. These results show that controller legality does not imply semantic validity, and that sound local recovery requires an explicit admissibility check.
2605.23311.paper_summary_zh: 當一個結構化工具代理在執行過程中失敗時，運行時面臨著一個兩難的局面：重播整個任務是安全的，但卻浪費資源，而從本地檢查點恢復則是高效的，但可能會使已經承諾的下游工作與不再存在的上游歷史相連。這種緊張在對承諾敏感的環境中尤為明顯，因為回滾針對的是單一失敗實例，而下游消費者已經對其輸出採取了行動。現有的恢復方法提供機械性的回滾，但沒有標準來判斷在下游承諾後，本地恢復是否仍然在語義上有效。我們將這一差距形式化為語義可恢復性，並在 DART 中解決它，這是一個模塊化的運行時，能夠定位失敗的實例，認證該實例的語義可恢復邊界，將檢查點對齊到這些邊界，並選擇一個在依賴性和效果約束下保留已承諾下游工作的可接受恢復點——否則則會阻止。通過三個 LLM 驅動的領域和基於 LangGraph 的基材的外部驗證，DART 正確地恢復了所有評估的對承諾敏感的案例，而基線本地恢復失敗，並且五個領域的安全審計未發現任何不安全的已承認回滾。這些結果表明控制器的合法性並不意味著語義有效性，並且健全的本地恢復需要明確的可接受性檢查。
2605.23311.author: Ke Yang et.al.
2605.23311.authors: Ke Yang, Panpan Li, Zonghan Wu, Kejin Xu, Huaxi Huang, Xiaoshui Huang
